item.title: Glimpses of a Vim configuration
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
It's been a whiôÙle sinôÙce I started traôÙcking verôÙsions of my cusôÙtom Vim conôÙfiôÙguôÙraôÙtioôÙn, and making it avaiôÙlaôÙble as an open sourôÙce soôÙftwaôÙre in GiôÙthub. ôÙThe best of this proôÙject is, in my opiôÙnioôÙn, to haôÙve it unôÙder verôÙsion conôÙtroôÙl, so I can traôÙck chanôÙges and reôÙleaôÙses.

EveôÙry onôÙce in a whiôÙle, when I find a new seôÙttinôÙg, or a great new feaôÙtuôÙre, I moôÙdiôÙfy the conôÙfiôÙguôÙraôÙtioôÙn, so they wiôÙll beôÙcoôÙme avaiôÙlaôÙble on the next reôÙleaôÙse. ôÙBeôÙsiôÙdes the feaôÙtuôÙres that are menôÙtioôÙned in the proôÙjecôÙt, and the cusôÙtoôÙmiôÙzaôÙtions maôÙde, I feel veôÙry coôÙmôÙforôÙtaôÙble wiôÙth the coôÙlour scheôÙme I maôÙde.

HeôÙre are soôÙme glimpôÙses of it:

First capôÙtuôÙre of coôÙlourôÙs, and laôÙyout

The coôÙlour scheôÙme is geôÙneôÙral for the synôÙtax hiôÙghôÙliôÙghôÙting of all tyôÙpes reôÙcogôÙniôÙzed by ViôÙm. PleaôÙse noôÙte this miôÙght alôÙso deôÙpend on the conôÙfiôÙguôÙraôÙtion of your terôÙmiôÙnaôÙl.

The taôÙbs are alôÙso theôÙmed acôÙcorôÙding to the meôÙnus.

Any suôÙggesôÙtions or imôÙproôÙveôÙmenôÙts to the coôÙde and conôÙfiôÙguôÙraôÙtion can be maôÙde on the GiôÙthub proôÙjecôÙt.

item.title: Checking Python files with Vim
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Vim is an exôÙceôÙllent ediôÙtoôÙr, and masôÙteôÙring it leaôÙds to moôÙre proôÙducôÙtiôÙviôÙty. Even thouôÙgh is veôÙrôÙy exôÙtenôÙsiôÙble and alloôÙws to be conôÙfiôÙguôÙred by many pluôÙg-ins I raôÙther keep it as simôÙple as poôÙsôÙsiôÙble, ôÙtrôÙying not to use many pluôÙg-ins (neiôÙther paôÙckaôÙgers like VundôÙle, etc.).

HoôÙweôÙveôÙr, I do make use of an exôÙtenôÙsion that cheôÙcks PyôÙthon fiôÙles for errorôÙs, PEôÙP8, aôÙmong other things: flake8. BeôÙcauôÙse I do not use pluôÙg-in plaôÙtforôÙms for ViôÙm, I insôÙtaôÙll just this one maôÙnuaôÙllôÙy, by making the coôÙmôÙmand flake8 avaiôÙlaôÙble sysôÙteôÙm-wiôÙde [1].

Then the insôÙtaôÙllaôÙtion is as simôÙple as doôÙwnôÙloading the proôÙject and coôÙying the fiôÙles inôÙto the ~/.ôÙôÙviôÙôÙm/ôÙôÙftpluôÙôÙgiôÙn/ôÙôÙpôÙôÙyôÙôÙthon diôÙrecôÙtoôÙrôÙy. Make suôÙre you haôÙve the foôÙlloôÙwing liôÙne added on your ~/.ôÙôÙviôÙôÙmrc:

filetype plugin indent on

The feaôÙtuôÙres I use are mainôÙly the synôÙtax and PEôÙôÙP-8 comôÙplianôÙce cheôÙckerôÙs. It can alôÙso warn you about unuôÙsôÙed imôÙporôÙts, and cyôÙcloôÙmaôÙtic comôÙpleôÙxiôÙty.

It is useôÙful beôÙcauôÙse things like PEôÙôÙP-8 comôÙplianôÙce help to haôÙve a good coôÙde quaôÙliôÙty, and theôÙreôÙfoôÙre a moôÙre reaôÙdaôÙble and mainôÙtaiôÙnaôÙble coôÙde baôÙse, speôÙciaôÙlly on larôÙge proôÙjecôÙts wiôÙth loôÙts of fiôÙles and moôÙduôÙleôÙs.

ThaôÙt's all. For moôÙre deôÙtails and other conôÙfiôÙguôÙraôÙtion tips cheôÙckout my Vim seôÙtup.

[1]Another option would be to install it on your virtual environment, but then you have to make sure to install it once per project. It is actually better, because you are not using the global system environment, but for packages like this, it should not be an issue, it's your choice.

item.title: Vim commands for improved productivity
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
I would like to desôÙcriôÙbe my faôÙvouôÙriôÙte Vim coôÙmôÙmanôÙds that I use on a daiôÙly baôÙsis, in orôÙder to shaôÙre soôÙme tips that could help you if you are new in this ediôÙtoôÙr, or ôÙto imôÙproôÙve your exôÙpeôÙrienôÙce even if you use it.

  • J : UseôÙful when orôÙgaôÙniôÙzing coôÙde, this wiôÙll joôÙin the liôÙne beôÙlow to the cuôÙrrent one.
  • ci) ("ôÙchanôÙge inôÙsiôÙde ')'): AcôÙtuaôÙllôÙy, the cloôÙsing braôÙcket could be chanôÙged by any other thinôÙg (ôÙlike ']', '}', etc.). This wiôÙll eraôÙse eveôÙrôÙyôÙthing wiôÙthin the braôÙckeôÙts and set you in inôÙsert moôÙde (ôÙthe c could alôÙso be chanôÙged for d for examôÙple if you just want to deôÙleôÙte). AgaiôÙn, this is ôÙveôÙry useôÙful when reôÙfacôÙtoôÙring coôÙde, if you want to chanôÙge the paôÙraôÙmeôÙters of a funcôÙtion deôÙfiôÙniôÙtioôÙn, or whateôÙver is in a bloôÙck, etc.
  • (sôÙelect soôÙme coôÙde wiôÙth viôÙsual moôÙde and then) zf : wiôÙll fold the seôÙlecôÙted coôÙde. zd for unôÙfolôÙdinôÙg.
  • % : aloôÙne or along wiôÙth soôÙme other opeôÙraôÙtoôÙr, is useôÙful for opeôÙraôÙting wiôÙth maôÙtchinôÙg ôÙbraôÙckeôÙts in the coôÙde. It wiôÙll maôÙtch the cloôÙsing braôÙcket of the one you haôÙve the curôÙsor in.
  • C or D : if you want to chanôÙge or deôÙleôÙte from the cuôÙrrent poôÙsiôÙtioôÙn up to the end of the liôÙne, resôÙpecôÙtiôÙveôÙlôÙy.
  • t, (or any other chaôÙracôÙter insôÙtead of coôÙmôÙma) wiôÙll poôÙint you until that chaôÙracôÙteôÙr. ôÙThe good about this, is that is poôÙsôÙsiôÙble to chain it wiôÙth other coôÙmôÙmanôÙdôÙs, for examôÙple: "ct," wiôÙll chanôÙge all the conôÙtent unôÙtil the next coôÙmôÙma.
  • < or > wiôÙll inôÙdent the coôÙde foôÙlloôÙwing the "aôÙrroôÙw" diôÙrecôÙtioôÙn (acôÙcorôÙding to what is set in shiôÙftwiôÙdth).
  • = AuôÙtoôÙmaôÙtiôÙcaôÙlly inôÙdenôÙts coôÙde (uôÙseôÙful when hiôÙghôÙliôÙghôÙting coôÙde in viôÙsual moôÙde).
  • w, e or b wiôÙll poôÙint you to the next worôÙd, to the end of the worôÙd, or back to the preôÙvious worôÙd, resôÙpecôÙtiôÙveôÙlôÙy. The niôÙce thing about theôÙse opeôÙraôÙtors is when they woôÙrk ôÙcomôÙbiôÙned wiôÙth otherôÙs, for examôÙple:
    • cw wiôÙôÙll chanôÙôÙge the next word.
    • db wiôÙôÙll deôÙôÙleôÙôÙte the preôÙôÙvious woôÙrôÙôÙd.
  • { or } for moôÙving up or doôÙwn thôÙrouôÙgh paôÙraôÙgraôÙphs, resôÙpecôÙtiôÙveôÙlôÙy.

In addiôÙtioôÙn, noôÙte that you do not need to know all poôÙsôÙsiôÙble coôÙmôÙmanôÙdôÙs, jusôÙt ôÙthoôÙse that wiôÙll help you wiôÙth your norôÙmal acôÙtiôÙviôÙtieôÙs. This means that is could be enouôÙgh wiôÙth a smaôÙll ôÙsubôÙset of all the feaôÙtuôÙres (the list I wroôÙte is veôÙry short inôÙdeeôÙd). And this is preôÙciôÙseôÙly the idea beôÙhind this posôÙt: to show hoôÙw ôÙsoôÙme few coôÙmôÙmanôÙds applied in the riôÙght conôÙtexôÙt, miôÙght make you edit fasôÙteôÙr.

All in all, Vim is a great ediôÙtoôÙr, fuôÙll of amaôÙzing feaôÙtuôÙres. LearôÙning it is acôÙtuaôÙlly worôÙth it, in the senôÙse that you wiôÙll get an amaôÙzing proôÙducôÙtiôÙviôÙty in reôÙturn (you'ôÙll tyôÙpe and ediôÙt ôÙcoôÙde fasôÙteôÙr).
